assets.description: Side-by-side PowerShell (left, primary) and Bash (right) panel showing the 6 exchanges: confirm the package manager, install tree (winget install GnuWin32.Tree on PowerShell, brew install tree on Bash), verify the new binary, make a practice-cli folder with a src subfolder, drop a README.md inside, and run tree on the result

PNG render of the same 6-exchange side-by-side panel. The PowerShell column uses winget install -e --id GnuWin32.Tree and invokes the new binary as tree.exe so the built-in tree.com in System32 does not win the PATH lookup; the Bash column uses brew install tree and invokes it as tree. The note strip under each window records the same cross-shell story
Typewriter animation of the 6-exchange PowerShell-primary walkthrough. Rendered with a 48-colour adaptive palette and typing_step=2 to stay well under the 2 MB gate

PowerShell-first screen-reader transcript of the 6-exchange session using the article's PATH-independent Windows commands (winget list for install verification, built-in tree /f for the outline), with a preamble that names how the transcript differs from the rendered visuals and a cross-shell takeaway that explains why Windows leans on the built-in tree.com when GnuWin32's bin folder is absent from PATH
